title: Barge system
abstract: A barge system (100) for offshore operations comprises a self-contained mother barge (101) with a deck (103) and two parallel hull elements (101a, 101b) extending rearwards to form a stern recess (102) in the mother barge (101). A feeder barge (110) may transport cargo between the mother barge (101) and shallow ports. The feeder barge (110) may be attached through a hinge mechanism (105) to support a jacket or tower during transport. Alternatively, pins (106) and grooves (107) may lock the feeder barge (110) the stern recess (102) during transport. The grooves (107) may permit vertical motion between the barges (101, 102), and the feeder barge (102) may be ballasted to form part of a lifting system. Some embodiments comprise several lifting towers (150) working together, e.g. for lifting a heavy topside.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A barge system (100) for offshore operations comprising a self-contained mother barge (101) with a deck (103) and two parallel hull elements (101a, 101b) extending rearwards to form a stern recess (102) in the mother barge (101), characterized by a releasable connection (105; 106, 107) suitable for attaching a feeder barge (110) in the stern recess (102) during transport to an offshore field and during offshore operations. 2. The barge system (100) according to claim 1, wherein the draft of the feeder barge (110) is less than the draft of the mother barge (101). 3. The barge system (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the releasable connection comprises a hinge mechanism (105) with an axis of rotation that extends across the stern recess (102) parallel to the deck (103). 4. The barge system (100) according to claim 3, further comprising a support arm (111) configured to pivot about the hinge mechanism (105). 5. The barge system (100) according to claim 4, wherein a partially submerged buoyancy element exert a buoyancy force on an aft end of the support arm (111). 6. The barge system (100) according to any preceding claim, wherein the releasable connection comprise retractable pins (106) configured to enter corresponding grooves (107). 7. The barge system (100) according to claim 6, wherein the grooves (107) extend perpendicular to the deck (103). 8. The barge system (100) according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the grooves (107) follow arcs of circles that are concentric with the hinge mechanism (105). 9. The barge system (100) according to any preceding claim, further comprising a conveyor system (130 - 133) for moving cargo (201, 202) on a deck (103, 113) without lifting the cargo (201, 202). 10. The barge system (100) according to any preceding claim, further comprising a lifting system (140, 145; 150) at the stern of the mother barge (101). 11. The barge system (100) according to claim 10, wherein the lifting system (140, 145; 150) comprises a main crane (140, 145). 12. The barge system (100) according to claim 10 or 11, wherein the lifting system (140, 145; 150) comprises a lifting tower (150). 13. The barge system (100) according to claim 12, wherein the lifting system (140, 145; 150) comprises at least two lifting towers (150) capable of lifting one structure (210, 220) together. 14. The barge system (100) according to claim 12 or 13, wherein the lifting tower(s) (150) are movable along the parallel hull elements (101a, 101b). 15. The barge system (100) according to any preceding claim, further comprising a dynamic positioning system.
